title: Former Biden official Deb Haaland wins New Mexico primary for governor
description: Haaland could make history as the first female Native American governor if she’s elected.

title: Hilton, Becerra lead in California’s governor primary. See live results here.
description: Latest California governor primary results as Democrats Katie Porter, Tom Steyer and Xavier Becerra and Republicans Chad Bianco, Steve Hilton vie for nominations to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom.

title: Senate Republicans pass immigration funding after overnight vote
description: After a marathon 18-hour vote, the Senate has funded immigration enforcement. The GOP bill funds ICE and the Border Patrol for three years.

title: Senate passes $70B immigration enforcement bill without limits on Trump settlement fund
description: The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.

title: The Supreme Court has left limited alternatives for protecting minority voting rights
description: After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.

title: Trump's choices tests the limits of Republican lawmakers' support
description: President Trump continues to pursue very personal agenda items that are testing the limits of support from Republican members of Congress.

title: Republican pollster shares how voters view Trump's agenda
description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Republican pollster Jim Hobart how voters view President Trump's agenda – the Iran war, the "anti-weaponization" fund, the ballroom – and what informs their midterm choices.

title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
description: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.

title: Republicans' sweeping election overhaul fails in the Senate
description: The SAVE America Act, a far-reaching Republican election overhaul that President Trump said should be his congressional allies' top priority, has failed in the Senate.

title: Trump's attacks on the press often focus on women reporters who challenge him
description: In an exchange with CNN's Kaitlin Collins, President Trump told her she had "hatred in her eyes." We look at the president's contentious relationship with the press and women reporters in particular.

title: Republican lawmaker on why he voted to end the war in Iran
description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Republican Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick about his decision to join Democrats and three other Republicans to vote to end the war in Iran.

title: The Trump administration infrequently backs down, but it did this week
description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks to The Atlantic's Anne Applebaum about why the Trump administration appears to be backing off some of its actions and what that means for concerns about democratic backsliding.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: The four Republicans who broke with Trump on Iran and now face his wrath
description: There are still hurdles before Congress could force the president to end hostilities, but the House resolution reflects lawmakers' growing impatience, including within Trump's own party.

title: California Democrats play it safe with Becerra, defying national trends
description: In the complicated governor’s race, voters embraced the former health secretary’s centrist stance over the passionate promises of insurgent candidates.

title: What to know about Trump’s immunity deal with the IRS
description: It’s “a legal absurdity,” said one expert.

title: Rep. Ogles deletes homophobic tweet, blames staff amid rare GOP pushback
description: A post on Ogles’s official X account Tuesday stated: “Homosexuality has no place in America. Happy Nuclear Family Month.”

title: Voters are sending a message to L.A.’s Democratic mayor
description: No incumbent mayor of the country's second-largest city has faced a runoff in more than two decades.

title: Pentagon hires convicted Jan. 6 rioter for sensitive counterterrorism job
description: The Trump administration’s appointment of Elias Irizarry, who was 19 at the time of the Capitol attack, has alarmed some in the Defense Department.

title: Nine takeaways from a big primary night in Iowa, California and more
description: In Iowa, Josh Turek won the Democratic nomination for Senate, defeating a rival who had criticized party leaders. Major races in California were not yet called.

title: Centrist Democrats launch new pledge: ‘We are capitalist, not socialist’
description: A pair of Democratic House members who won Republican-held seats want others to sign a vow for moderate policies.
